issues.description: Script performs fetch requests to Telegram's own domains to download media content. No evidence of data being sent to third-party servers or user data leakage.
issues.recommendation: Ensure fetch requests are only made to trusted Telegram domains and do not include sensitive user data in requests.

issues.description: Script reads no cookies, localStorage, or sessionStorage. No event listeners for keyboard input or form data reading detected. No suspicious fingerprinting API usage found.
issues.location: Global script scope and event listeners
issues.recommendation: No action needed as no privacy invasive data collection detected.

issues.description: No usage of eval(), new Function(), setTimeout(string), or innerHTML with untrusted content detected. No dynamic script injection or @require of remote scripts found.
issues.recommendation: Maintain current practice of avoiding remote code execution vectors.

issues.description: No @grant directives found in metadata, so no permission over-privilege detected.
No usage of sensitive APIs such as navigator.geolocation, RTCPeerConnection, MediaDevices, or Clipboard API detected.
No evidence of code obfuscation, base64 decoding, or suspicious string concatenation for code execution found.
No external @require dependencies found in metadata, all code is self-contained. URLs used for update and download are from greasyfork.org, a known script repository.
issues.recommendation: Ensure update URLs are from trusted sources and monitor for supply chain risks.

summary: The script is designed to download media from Telegram webapp private channels by fetching media content directly from Telegram's own domains. It does not collect or transmit user data to third parties, nor does it use privacy-invasive APIs or remote code execution techniques. No permission over-privilege or suspicious external dependencies are present. Overall, the script poses a low security risk.
